hawkish_phrases: risks to the inflation outlook were now tilted to the upside
services inflation had been stuck at roughly 4% for more than a year
upside risks could delay a sustainable return to target
greater caution was needed on the size and pace of further rate cuts when policy rates were approaching neutral territory
high levels of uncertainty, lingering upside risks to energy and food prices, a strong labour market and high negotiated wage increases, as well as sticky services inflation, called for caution
dovish_phrases: disinflation process remained well on track
inflation had continued to develop broadly in line with the staff projections
most measures of underlying inflation suggested that inflation would settle around the target on a sustained basis
risks to economic growth remained tilted to the downside
as long as the disinflation process remained on track, policy rates could be brought further towards a neutral level to avoid unnecessarily holding back the economy
summary: The ECB cut rates by 25 basis points unanimously, with disinflation on track but concerns expressed about sticky services inflation at 4%, upside inflation risks, and the need for caution as rates approach neutral territory despite downside growth risks.
